commit.message:
feat(model-3d): add face splitting to Circle and Arc tools

Both tools now split existing HEMesh faces when drawing on them,
matching the Rectangle tool's behavior. Circle generates a 32-point
N-gon polygon; Arc uses its curve points as a closed polygon (arc +
chord). Falls back to standalone 3D geometry on ground plane.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): preserve heFaceIds in cloneFaceData for group push-pull

cloneFaceData was copying heFaceId (singular) but dropping heFaceIds
(the group array), so extrudeFaceGroup was never called during push-pull.

commit.message:
fix: PostHog proxy 404 on locale-prefixed ingest paths

With localePrefix: 'always', PostHog calls /fr/ingest/e, /de/ingest/flags
etc. which didn't match the rewrite rules (only /ingest/* was handled).
Added locale-prefixed rewrite patterns for all 10 locales.

commit.message:
feat: add diagnostic_mode to identify register→display mapping (v2.6.24)

Writes unique fingerprint values to each key register:
  30071 → 1111,  32064 → 2222,  32080 → 3333
  37113 → 4444,  37132 → 5555,  37760 → 666 (66.6% SOC),  37765 → 7777
Enable diagnostic_mode: true, check Nibe display to match values to registers.

commit.message:
fix: increase gpu-memory-utilization to 0.6 for 14B model

The 14B model requires ~30GB to load, leaving negative KV cache memory
at 0.4 utilization (~32GB on H100). Bumping to 0.6 (~48GB) provides
sufficient headroom for KV cache and LoRA adapters.

fix(pokemon): auto-handle force-switch and disabled moves for any agent

Instead of returning 400 when an agent sends a move command during
force-switch or picks a disabled/exhausted move, the server now
auto-corrects silently:
- Force-switch: auto-switch to first alive Pokemon slot
- Disabled/zero-PP move: auto-pick first valid available move

Prevents games from freezing when agents don't handle these edge cases.

commit.message:
Add majority voting for MCQ answer "1" detection

When expected answer is "1", run 3 parallel detections at temperatures
0.3, 0.5, 0.7 and take the majority (2+ of 3 agree). Applies to both
the retry pass during batch marking and remarkSingleQuestion.

"1" is a single vertical stroke that AI frequently misses or misreads,
so multiple attempts with varied temperatures gives more reliable results.

commit.message:
Fix critical, high, and medium security vulnerabilities (#160)

## Summary

Comprehensive security audit and fixes for 17 vulnerabilities across
backend, collab server, frontend, and CI/CD:

**Critical (C1–C3):**
- Add `requirePermission` middleware to PDF upload/download endpoints
(IDOR fix)
- Fix yjs_document deletion using correct column name + add FK cascade
constraint

**High (H1–H6):**
- Sanitize PDF filenames to prevent path traversal
- Enforce 15MB PDF upload size limit
- Add FK constraint from `yjs_document.room_name` → `document(id)` with
CASCADE
- Default CORS to deny-all when `ALLOWED_ORIGIN` is unset + validate
WebSocket Origin
- Add `permissions: contents: read` to all GitHub Actions workflows
- Pin all GitHub Actions to immutable commit SHAs

**Medium (M1–M14, M17–M18):**
- Validate JWT `alg` header (reject non-HS256) and `iss`/`aud` claims
- Enforce max 90-day share link expiry, reject null/empty dates
- Verify document permission before issuing ws-ticket JWT
- Add WebSocket message size limit (256KB) and connection cap (50/room)
- Verify folder ownership before moving documents
- Expand `.gitignore` to cover `.env.*` variants
- Add permission check to tag DELETE endpoint
- Remove `ws:` from CSP `connect-src` (only allow `wss:`)
- Self-host PDF.js worker instead of loading from unpkg CDN
- Update dompurify and hono to patch known CVEs

## Test plan

- [x] Backend TypeScript compiles with zero errors
- [x] Collab server tests pass (13/13)
- [x] Frontend build succeeds
- [x] Frontend unit tests pass (2040/2040)
- [x] Frontend lint clean
- [x] E2E tests: 6 pass, 2 fail (pre-existing arrow drawing failures
identical on main)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(2.4a): calibrate translation verifier — 9/9 tests pass

Story 2.4a: Calibrate Translation Verifier against 10-event German
test set (5 good, 5 planted errors). All 5 error types detected:
- T0: stale baseEnVersion (automated structural check)
- T1: untranslated string still in English (AI verifier)
- T3: wrong year in translated description (AI verifier)
- T5: tone/formality mismatch — informal slang (AI verifier)
- T9: stripped diacritics — ASCII substitutions (automated check)

Zero false positives on known-good events. Portuguese diacritics
PostToolUse hook implemented and tested (rejects stripped, allows correct).

Translation verifier prompt committed to Scripts/sdk/prompts/translation-verifier.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix video mode: fire-and-forget + Supabase polling + thumbnail capture

- API route: video modunda fire-and-forget (anında 202 döner, timeout yok)
- Frontend: video tamamlanana kadar Supabase'i poll eder (2s × 300 = 10 dk)
- Overall timeout video için 7 dakikaya çekildi
- fetchLatestAssets: job_id ve URL pattern fallback ile video asset bulma güçlendirildi
- captureVideoFirstFrame: preload=auto, canplay event, resolved flag ile güvenilir hale getirildi
- next.config.mjs: media-src direktifi eklendi (Cloudflare Worker video URL'leri için)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ted): bypass Render server for YouTube RSS with client-side CORS proxy fallback

YouTube RSS feed is blocked from Render's cloud IPs, causing the TED
video list to fail. Fix:
- Primary: backend /api/ted-videos (8s timeout)
- Fallback: client-side fetch via allorigins.win CORS proxy + DOMParser
Both paths produce the same video object format. Error shown only if both fail.

- xlwings==0.33.20: Windows/macOS Excel desktop automation, not a server

    apply_rtl_styles(lang): injects RTL CSS (html/body, inputs, textareas,
      selects, .stMarkdown, .stForm, flex row-reverse for columns, sidebar
      right-positioned, buttons right-aligned) via st.markdown when
      is_rtl(lang) is True; no-op for LTR languages.
    get_rtl_column_order(cols): reverses column list for RTL session language.
    rtl_text(text, lang): wraps text in direction:rtl span for RTL; returns
      plain text for LTR.

RTL languages covered: ar, ur, ps, fa, ku, he, sd.
All 276 tests pass (217 existing + 59 new).

* feat(security): Module 4 — hCaptcha integration

- Add app/security/captcha.py:
    verify_hcaptcha(token, secret): POSTs to hCaptcha siteverify API,
      returns True on success, False on failure/exception/empty args.
      Never raises. Handles network errors, timeouts, malformed responses.
    is_captcha_required(role): returns False for SystemAdmin (via
      has_permission() check on sysadmin.panel.access), True for all
      other roles and unauthenticated users (None).
    get_hcaptcha_site_key(): reads HCAPTCHA_SITE_KEY env var.
    get_hcaptcha_secret(): reads HCAPTCHA_SECRET env var.

- Add app/ui/captcha_widget.py:
    render_captcha_placeholder(lang): st.info banner + st.text_input
      for token paste. All labels via t(). Streamlit-phase implementation;
      full browser widget in Module 15 (React frontend).
    get_captcha_token_from_session(): returns token from session state.
    clear_captcha_token(): resets token after use.

- Modify app/services/auth_service.py: add captcha_token and captcha_role
  params to register() and login(). CAPTCHA enforced when HCAPTCHA_SECRET
  is set; SystemAdmin bypasses; raises AuthError("captcha_invalid") on
  failure.

* fix(branding): remove permission_matrix import from UI page

Removed direct import of has_permission from app.roles.permission_matrix
and PermissionDeniedError from app.services.role_service in
app/ui/pages/branding_page.py.

The display gate now uses a role-string check (role != "SystemAdmin")
which is a UI-only guard — not a security boundary.  The actual
permission enforcement remains inside BrandingService.update_config()
via its injected RoleService (sysadmin.branding.manage).

Fixes Architecture Compliance CI rule:
  "Verify no permission_matrix imports in UI pages"

* fix(arch): remove all permission_matrix imports from UI layer

Audit of app/ui/ found four pages with live imports from
app.roles.permission_matrix, violating CLAUDE_RULES.md rule:
"Verify no permission_matrix imports in UI pages".

Files fixed:
- notification_prefs_page.py: removed has_permission import;
  replaced has_permission(role, "admin.panel.access") with
  role in ("Admin", "SystemAdmin")
- workday_ats_page.py: removed has_permission import;
  replaced has_permission(role, "ats.workday") with
  role not in ("Admin", "SystemAdmin")
- analytics_page.py: removed has_permission import;
  replaced 3 calls — admin.metrics.view, coach.view_assigned_users,
  admin.export.reports (×2) — with equivalent role-string checks
- permission_console_page.py: replaced two inline
  `from app.roles.permission_matrix import …` with calls to new
  PermissionService.list_roles() and list_all_permissions() helpers

Also added list_roles() and list_all_permissions() to PermissionService
(which already held PERMISSIONS and ALL_PERMISSIONS) so the console
page can delegate via the service layer without touching the matrix.

commit.message:
Add GitHub Actions background cron scan

- Runs every 30 minutes via GitHub Actions schedule
- Fetches top movers from Finnhub (US, Europe, Asia, Crypto, Commodities)
- Analyzes each with GPT-4o

commit.message:
refactor: independent weight evaluation — Sonnet blind review, no Haiku prompt injection

- Sonnet evaluates task weights WITHOUT seeing current weights (blind assessment)
- Removed weight calibration/few-shot injection from Haiku's classifier prompt
- Added get_weight_calibration_hints() in db.py for analytics (not injected)
- Programmatic comparison: code detects divergence, stores in weight_feedback
- Architecture: Haiku assigns draft weights → Sonnet corrects nightly → clean prompt

- Add OpenAIResponsesProcessor for Responses API SSE event format
- Always use Responses API for OpenAI provider (newer, recommended API)
- Support native computer use tool for GPT-5.4+
- Support batched actions execution in computer tool handler
- Add previous_response_id chaining for efficient multi-turn conversations
- Add GPT-5.4, Gemini 3.1 Pro/Flash models to providers list
- Auto-register native computer tool for OpenAI when operator mode enabled

feat: add all Metered TURN relay endpoints for robust NAT traversal

behind restrictive firewalls and mobile networks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: switch to 1 uvicorn worker to prevent OOM crashes

2 workers × (Whisper 2GB + resemblyzer 0.5GB) = 5GB > 4GB container limit.
With async IngestWorker (asyncio.to_thread), 1 worker handles everything
without blocking the event loop. No more Child process died.
